title: AccessGrant
description: One per-resource dynamic access grant. Polymorphic `subject` (the principal receiving the permission — usually a User, potentially a Group in future) and polymorphic `resource` (the target the grant applies to — Invoice, Athlete, Team, any domain aggregate that composes IsGrantable + is registered in the GrantableRegistry). The `permissions` JSONB array names the specific permissions being granted, scoped to this exact (resource_type, resource_id) tuple. `decision` splits allow-vs-deny — deny wins over allow AND over RBAC roles, and short-circuits every permission check. `expires_at` is nullable (permanent grant; enterprise-gated) but observer refuses past-dated values. `source` traces where the grant came from (manual admin CRUD / access-request approval / delegation / bulk provision) with `source_reference` naming the specific approval / delegation / bulk-batch row.

tenant_id.description: Owning tenant. Auto-filled by BelongsToTenant. Grants are STRICTLY tenant-scoped — cross-tenant refusal at the observer + rule layer.

subject_type.description: Polymorphic subject key. Registered kinds today: `user`. Future: `group`. Enforced by `grantable_subject_type` rule.

subject_id.description: ID of the principal receiving the grant. Not FK-constrained at DB level (polymorphic) — existence verified at write time by the GrantResourceValidator.

resource_type.description: Polymorphic resource key registered via GrantableRegistry (e.g. `invoice`, `athlete`, `team`, `document`). Enforced by the `grantable_resource_registered` validation rule.

resource_id.description: ID of the resource row. Not FK-constrained at DB level (polymorphic) — existence verified at write time by GrantResourceValidator. ReconcileOrphanedGrantsJob sweeps periodically to catch resources that disappeared out-of-band.

permissions.description: JSONB array of permission NAME strings scoped to this specific (resource_type, resource_id). No scope suffix required — the resource IS the scope. Each name is cross-referenced against the `permissions` table (owned by access/rbac) at write time by PermissionCatalogueValidator; unknown names refused with PERMISSION_UNKNOWN.

decision.description: Enum: `allow` (positive grant) or `deny` (inviolable block). Deny wins over allow AND over RBAC roles — the safety guarantee we sell. Enterprise-gated (see `deny_grants` entitlement).

granted_by.description: The User who issued this grant. Nullable because system-generated grants (`source='access_request'` where the approval was auto-approved, `source='bulk_provision'` for CSV batches) may have no direct human grantor — the audit trail lives on the source_reference row instead.

granted_at.description: Moment the grant became effective. Defaults to `now()` on create. Distinct from `created_at` — an admin may back-date `granted_at` to match a paper trail (observer refuses future values).

expires_at.description: Nullable. NULL = permanent grant (enterprise-gated via `permanent_grants` entitlement; other tiers force expires_at ≤ now() + 365d). Observer refuses `expires_at < now()` at create. `PurgeExpiredGrantsJob` sweeps periodically and transitions past-expires_at rows to `revoked` state (semantically) by setting `revoked_at = expires_at` + `revoked_reason = 'expired'`.

revoked_at.description: When the grant was revoked (or auto-revoked due to expiry / orphan). NULL for active grants. When set, the row is considered terminal and never appears in resolver lookups (partial index excludes non-NULL).

revoked_by.description: User who revoked. NULL for system-driven revokes (expiration sweep, orphan reconciliation).

revoked_reason.description: Free-text reason for revocation. Required when the revoker is a User (admin CRUD, delete). System-driven revokes populate this with the sentinel `'expired'` / `'orphaned_resource'` / `'orphaned_subject'`.

reason.description: Mandatory operator note. Minimum 20 characters (enforced by `valid_grant_reason` rule + settings). The audit-trail justification for the grant. Retained INDEFINITELY for audit even after grant expiry / revoke — never redacted.

source.description: Enum: `manual` (admin CRUD), `access_request` (materialised from a workflow/approvals instance), `delegation` (issued via access/delegation flow — reserved for Wave 1b delegation module), `bulk_provision` (CSV batch upload).

source_reference.description: The ID of the source row that produced this grant. When source='access_request': the approval_instance_id (`api_<26>`). When source='bulk_provision': the batch id. When source='delegation': the delegation_id. NULL for source='manual'. Powers reverse-lookup (`grants:audit-source api_...` → list grants materialised from this approval).

metadata.description: From HasMetadata. Free-form platform notes bag. Typical contents: `csv_row_index` for bulk, `approver_notes` mirrored from approval, `delegation_scope` for delegation-sourced grants.

indexes.name: access_grants_resolver_hot_idx
indexes.predicate: revoked_at IS NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL
indexes.description: The overlay resolver's primary read path. Covers the (principal, resource, decision) tuple every permission check touches; the partial index means only active rows are scanned. Deny-first behaviour reads with `decision='deny'` first, then `decision='allow'`.

indexes.name: access_grants_expiration_scan_idx
indexes.predicate: revoked_at IS NULL AND expires_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL
indexes.description: Powers PurgeExpiredGrantsJob (hourly) + NotifyGrantExpiringJob (daily). Ordered scan of grants about to expire.

indexes.name: access_grants_source_reverse_idx
indexes.predicate: source_reference IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL
indexes.description: Reverse-lookup from approval / delegation / bulk-batch → grants. Consumers: workflow/approvals' 'grants issued from this approval' panel.

indexes.name: access_grants_grantor_scan_idx
indexes.predicate: granted_by IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL
indexes.description: Powers 'grants issued by this admin' audit view.

permissions.description: 1-64 unique permission-name strings. Each cross-referenced against the `permissions` table by PermissionCatalogueValidator at write time.

is_active.description: Computed. revoked_at IS NULL AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now()).

is_expired.description: Computed. expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at <= now() AND revoked_at IS NULL.

is_permanent.description: Computed. expires_at IS NULL (permanent grant — enterprise-gated).
